content.full_text:
PACIFIC COMMISSION

EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

75-10-129

October 3, 1975

Mr. Jon Mayhew
Legislative Representative
California State Council
Service Employees International Union, AFL-CIO
1220 H Street, Suite 202
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 75-177

Dear Mr. Mayhew:

Thank you for your letter of September 17,
1975, requesting an opinion with respect to restrictions on lobbyists' contributions under the Political
Reform Act. Since your letter raises no substantial
question involving an interpretation of the Act,
your request for a formal opinion is denied.

One purpose of the Political Reform Act is
to remove the influence of money from the relationship between state officeholders and lobbyists. The
fact that a state officeholder decides to seek another
office while still an incumbent does not eliminate the
need to enforce provisions designed to further this
purpose. Accordingly, contributions to the state officeholder continue to be prohibited.

I hope the foregoing is helpful. If you have
any further questions, please contact Ted Prin of
the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman, for the
Commission.

DHL:jp

Date Received 9.22.75
F_________ 12-6-75

CALIFORNIA STATE COUNCIL
SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, AFL-CIO

75/121

September 17, 1975

Dan Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1190 K St.
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

Dear Chairman Lowenstein,

As a lobbyist, I understand that I cannot make personal contributions
to any candidate for state office. However, Proposition 9 apparently
does not apply to campaigns for local office.

I have contributed to a candidate for Mayor of Sacramento, as well as
various City Council candidates. I understand to be quite legal.
However, I would also like to make personal contributions to a candidate
for Mayor of San Francisco, who happens to be a State Senator.

I am requesting a ruling from the Commission on the legality of such
a contribution. Since both races are local and non-partisan, I cannot
understand why I can legally contribute to the campaign of a mayor for
one city, but not another, if such is the case.

Very truly yours,

Jon Mayhew
Jon Mayhew, Legislative Representative

Legislative Office · 1220 H Street, Suite 202 · Sacramento, CA 95811 · (916) 442-3933
Main Office · 240 Golden Gate Avenue, Room 331 · San Francisco, CA 94102 · (415) 441-2350

JON MAYHEW          THOMAS P. COLEMAN          TIMOTHY J. TWOMEY
